name: Walt Pearson
description: An old man on a porch step turning a ring of picks over in his hands, watching the cul-de-sac with the patience of someone who has nowhere left to be.
background: Neighbourhood locksmith for forty years; there is not a door on Maple Street he cannot open.
personality: Dry, watchful, and slow to warm — he measures a person before he'll hand over a key.
secret: He can't sleep in his own house anymore, so he lets himself into the neighbours' empty ones — and takes one small keepsake from each, a grief he calls tidying up.
notes: T45 · The Terraces. Skill: locks/entry. Hook: access to sealed homes/caches (FR-NPC-06); the quiet theft is grief, not menace.
